summary: Clean soundboard capture of a rain‑soaked 1985 show highlighted by a soaring Bird Song > Comes a Time > Deal sequence, stellar Morning Dew and an epic Music Never Stopped jam.
review: The Braverman SBD delivers crystal‑clear audio despite the downpour, letting the band’s high‑energy performance shine. Highlights include the Bird Song > Comes a Time > Deal trifecta, a transcendent Morning Dew and a jaw‑dropping Healy Sound Attack → Music Never Stopped jam, while Jerry’s vocals show strain but his guitar work remains superb.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4‑5 stars praising both the performance and recording; a single low rating concerns venue security, not the music, yielding an overall strong but slightly moderated score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Consistently described as clean, clear and crisp with no reported drop‑outs; the mix holds up well even under heavy rain.

standout_songs: Bird Song > Comes A Time > Deal
Healy Sound Attack > Music Never Stopped
show_quality.setlist_flow: A bizarre, rain‑laden first set gives way to a second set that erupts with improvisational fireworks and strong vocal/ instrumental moments despite Jerry’s health issues.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocally strained due to health problems but guitar playing described as stellar throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Phil: Delivers a powerful vocal on Tom Thumb's Blues; his bass even blows a speaker during Morning Dew, underscoring his intensity.
